title: geum pusillum
intro: Geum pusillum, also known as the small avens or prairie geum, is a perennial plant species belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). It is native to the prairies of North America and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers are used in herbal preparations to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, skin irritations, and respiratory infections, and are also believed to have antioxidant and immune-boosting properties. Geum pusillum can be consumed as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, or used topically as a salve or poultice, offering a range of health benefits and medicinal uses.
